entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The grain legume crop studied for hybrid vigour and molecular diversity.

entities.name: HYBRID VIGOUR
entities.description: The superior performance of hybrid offspring compared to their parents.

entities.name: HETEROSIS
entities.description: The genetic phenomenon where hybrid offspring exhibit superior traits over parents.

entities.name: HETEROBELTIOSIS
entities.description: A measure of heterosis where the hybrid is superior to the better parent.

entities.name: RELATIVE HETEROSIS
entities.description: A measure of heterosis relative to the better parent.

entities.name: ADDITIVE GENETIC VARIATION
entities.description: Genetic variation that contributes linearly to the phenotype of the offspring.

entities.name: NON-ADDITIVE GENETIC VARIATION
entities.description: Genetic variation involving dominance or epistasis contributing to hybrid vigour.

entities.name: MOLECULAR DIVERSITY
entities.description: The degree of genetic variation at the molecular level among parents.

entities.name: HETEROTIC GROUPS
entities.description: Groups of parents that produce superior hybrids when crossed.

entities.name: A-LINES
entities.description: Specific breeding lines used to form heterotic groups.

entities.name: R-LINES
entities.description: Specific breeding lines used to form heterotic groups.

entities.name: ICPA 2209
entities.description: A specific parent line of pigeonpea used in hybrid breeding.

entities.name: ICPL 20108
entities.description: A specific parent line of pigeonpea used in hybrid breeding.
